I I'll switch over to a demo in a moment
but first I want to make it uh really
clear what I mean here the reporting I'm
about to show you can map directly to
your organizational structure so here on

two business units with multiple
divisions underneath and projects that
contribute to these divisions uh the
portfolio reporting functionality of
star cube can directly match this
portfolio structure uh and give you some
really powerful insights so what I'm
showing you here is actually the
administration console for constructing
a portfolio in sonar Cube uh this page
lets you see what your portfolio looks
like at a high level and also how
projects have been assigned to various
portfolios strictly speaking uh the only
portfolio here is at the top level keing
John's Corp and everything underneath
that is considered a sub portfolio which
will be important here in a second I
have a lot of options uh for how I want
to add projects to my portfolios I can
do that manually just by selecting
individual projects I can decide to
include projects in my portfolios based
on uh tags that project administrators
have added to the projects uh or I could
choose to include projects by regular
expression so if I have project keys
that always follow a specific format I
can set a regular expression to make
sure that all of those projects get
brought into the portfolio and if needed
I can also add all remaining projects
here in this portfolio I've just added
projects manually uh I've added the main
branch of all these projects but you can
also add specific branches and you can
add the same project multiple times to
these portfolios with different branches
uh and here I have my top level
organization I have three uh business
units under that customer acquisition
operations and R&D and then some
departments that go uh underneath that
and I decided to assign all of my
projects at the the very lowest
level uh and so now that all of uh this
portfolio is configured we can actually
come back and view the portfolio which
is showing the aggregate of all of these
projects that have been included in the
portfolio hierarchy uh so you can see
that there are 22 projects in this
portfolio I have an overview of all of
these projects uh and can see which
projects currently have a passing
quality gate and which ones don't this
is the top level View and so I can also
see a breakdown of the main axes of
sonar Cube's quality model so
reliability maintainability security and
Security review so this is the top level
showing all of my projects but I can
also move into this portfolio breakdown
uh which starts to drill down into the
various business units that I've have to
finded in my portfolio hierarchy so I
can see here that uh R&D has an e rating
for security and if I want to learn more
I can click into here and start getting
a better idea of where those issues are
uh in my organization but maybe I just
want to see a uh a single security
report for the entire R&D organization
uh of my company that same security
report that I was talking about for uh
individual projects can also be found at
the portfolio level uh so I can really
get an understanding of what kinds of
issues what kind of security issues this
department is facing
specifically now if I want to get a real
idea about where there's some risk in my
organization I can also come over to the
measures Tab and start looking at some
of the visualizations that are offered
to me in this portfolio so what this
port what the specific graph is looking
at is uh where do I have low code
coverage um and how much technical debt
do I have um so these projects down here
okay very little technical debt uh and
an acceptable amount of code coverage
but I can really start to drill into
these projects to understand all right
something is clearly not going too well
here uh and if I want to look at the
progress of all the projects in my
portol folio over time I also have this
activity tab now I created this
portfolio yesterday so there's not a lot
of history here but if I come over to
another instance of sonar Cube that
includes all of the projects in that
instance in one portfolio I can look at
something like code coverage and see hey
over time is the amount of coverage for
projects in my organization increasing
decreasing here it looks like it's uh
just been increasing so you know if my
company had an initiative to improve
code coverage for example I can be
pretty confident that that initiative
was working uh really portfolios give
you a lot of insights uh at a high level
of what's going on in your organization
while still letting you drill down to
the right granularity whether that's a
uh business unit or division department
or an individual project and figure out
you know what's going on in my
organization these dashboards are live
every time that there's a new analysis
of a project the portfolio gets computed
so these are always up to date you don't
have to wait until these uh
dashboards rerun rerun every morning or
something they're just always um just
always up to date
